content:
Welcome back folks.
We are doing a short market review of the Dollar Index, Euro Dollar, S&P, and NASDAQ futures.

The Dollar Index weekly chart showed a point of consideration for a discount last week. While it looked like
it touched, it was one pipette short of touching the high, which is the actual low of this weekly candle,
represented by its date.
Currently, we are working up into this area, which is a sell side imbalance, buy side inefficiency (SIBI/
BSIE). I am watching how we trade in this area.

- KEY PRICE POINTS TO OBSERVE
I am keeping a close eye on two price points:
- The consequent encroachment of this candle's wick (midpoint).
- The midpoint of this other wick.
Extend those two out on your own chart. How we trade, if at all, in either of these areas will be interesting
to me.

On the daily chart, we got real close to the target but didn't quite reach it. Now, price has pulled up into this
inefficiency. There's a small gap, which is actually a real liquidity void where no actual trading was printed
between these two respective candles (this candle's high, next candle's low).
It will be interesting to see if it wants to reprice into this little gap. If it stays open and starts to trade lower,
this can be something that can stay open for a long time.

- VOLUME IMBALANCE DEFINITION
On the Euro Dollar weekly chart, we observe a weekly volume imbalance. This is the difference between
this candle's closing price and this candle's opening price. While we did have this candlestick's tail and this
candlestick's wick overlap, I've drawn your attention to this ahead of time, having talked about it for several
months.
The difference between those two price points is that there is no body, no candlestick body, on this time
frame. When a market shows an unwillingness to drop a body between two respective candlesticks, that is
a volume imbalance. I dub it a volume imbalance because that time frame needs to be efficiently delivered.

- LIQUIDITY DELIVERY & PRICE ACTION
The algorithm will refer back to the weekly time frame, seeing an inefficiency here. Everyone else would not
see this as an inefficiency because they see that it traded back and forth, but that's not efficient.
The draw on liquidity that we've been looking for for months has finally been delivered.
The high of the week opened, traded up just to the high of that inefficiency (the weekly volume imbalance),
and then traded down near the close of this volume imbalance on the low end. So, we had the high end of
it and the low end of it, and the close was just about right at the low. We traded up, met the high of it, and
then closed back down.

- CONFIRMING PRICE ACTION AND CONCEPT VALIDITY
On the daily chart for the Euro Dollar, you can see how we did in fact deliver here and painted right back
down over top of that range from the low of the weekly volume imbalance up to the high and then back
down again.
We had several targets from the July 9th analysis video, which you can find on YouTube. This is where it
began before this candlestick started trading. We were looking for a move back down into the weekly
inversion gap, then a draw to buy side (which it did here), and then a draw up into the weekly volume
imbalance. This completes a rather obvious illustration of what my concepts can do for you, what you're
looking for in terms of precision, and why the price should behave a specific way.

- FORWARD OUTLOOK & INTRADAY TRADING STRATEGY
At this juncture, it's important to realize that we don't have to pick the next move right now. In fact, it can go
both directions:
- It can go higher to trade up into this Fair Value Gap.
- Or it can dig deeper into this inefficiency here (a buy side imbalance, sell side inefficiency
- BISI) or this
SIBI (sell side imbalance, buy side inefficiency).
Either one of those can be traded to. Because of the 50/50 likelihood, we have to drop down below the
daily chart and be a day trader, trading the intraday sessions such as the London Open Session and the
New York Open Session.

- IDENTIFYING INEFFICIENCIES
On the hourly chart for the Euro Dollar, price traded through the buy side, came up, found some support at
the low end of that weekly volume imbalance, then traded all the way to the high. It then broke down and
aggressively returned to the low of the weekly volume imbalance.
On the upside, you want to have:
- This inefficiency.
- And this inefficiency.
Both of those are areas of interest for me, in terms of premium and/or discount, respectively.

- WAITING FOR WEEKLY RANGE CONFIRMATION
I don't want to be overzealous and point to something up here or something down here, because I don't
have Sunday's opening price yet. Right now, it's a little after four o'clock my local time. I'm keeping my cards
close to my vest this week and will allow Monday's trading to give me more insights about the weekly range.
It's okay for you not to know, because sometimes I don't know. I never know what it's going to do when we
open up on Sunday. Sunday's opening price is a mystery, it's absolutely unknowable. You have to submit
yourself to not knowing what it is and then watching and seeing how we behave after that new week
opening.

- PREVIOUS WEEK'S ANALYSIS REVIEW
On the S&P weekly chart (left), and NASDAQ mini futures weekly chart (right):
I mentioned that this inefficiency here would be a likely culprit to draw up into. We saw that last week. This
was the initial one, which we saw price trade to and then through it. There was also a Fair Value Gap that
we had an interest in.
On the NASDAQ weekly chart, I mentioned this is likely to be the upside draw as well, and we got that
delivered last week, off the heels of the weekly Fair Value Gap here in the discount, which sent price higher.

- INTERMEDIATE TERM HIGH CONDITIONS
While I'm not trying to call the top of the marketplace, it's in an area where I think it's reasonable to see it
come back down into this inefficiency (for S&P) and over here (for NASDAQ) respectively.
If we take out the down-closed candle on the weekly basis (if we take out that low), then I would submit to
the idea that we're probably creating an intermediate term high on the weekly chart for S&P. Same thing
here for NASDAQ, if we trade below the down-closed candle on a closing basis.
This is important: it has to be on a closing basis for both S&P and NASDAQ. If it were to close below this
candle's low, then I would likely assume that we are creating an intermediate term high, not that it's the top,
not that it can't go higher later in the year. It just means for right now it would change gears for me, and
then I would likely look for lower time frame sell setups and focus there until I was shown that the market
did in fact want to return back to being bullish. But right now, everything's still bullish. Nothing here indicates
there's a top.

- SHORT-TERM BEARISH OUTLOOK
On the S&P daily chart (left side), we have an immediate rebalance, which is this candle and then the next
down-closed candle. We opened, traded up to the previous up-closed candle, and then rejected that. So,
unless it takes out Friday's High, I'm looking for the likelihood of it drawing back down into this inefficiency
here because we didn't completely close it in.
It can trade down there and still be bullish longer term. It doesn't need to change anything by doing that
because what we have here is a swing low, and trailed stop losses have been brought right below that low.
We have a little bit of inefficiency here between this candle's high and this candle's low, so it could trade
down there and still not upset anything.

- SPECIFIC TARGETS AND REVERSAL CONDITIONS
Essentially, that's the midpoint of that lower inefficiency on the weekly chart. So, it can trade down here,
quick below this close, close this entirely, and even go so far as to get down to the consequent
encroachment from this wick. I'd like to see this area down here stay open as a breakaway gap.
All of that changes if we trade on a closing basis above Friday's High.

- EXPECTED MOVEMENT AND ORDER BLOCK
Same thing here on NASDAQ. We have almost entirely closed in this Fair Value Gap, but I'd like to see it
trade down into this area here, into this order block.
I'm expecting and anticipating, and for my own analysis, predicting that we would likely trade down more
into this inefficiency. Now, if we gap down, create a gap opening, trade up, and then go lower, then
obviously that might require a little bit more movement into this order block, maybe mean threshold right in
here.

- PRE-MARKET OPENING CAUTION
It's a lot of wait and see, and it's always the case before we open up on Sunday. I can have a lot of different
scenarios that I would favor, or I would like to see, or my concern would be around a specific scenario
panning out in price on Sunday's opening price going into Monday's trading. But all of that is just for the
purpose of studying, because I don't trade on Sundays anymore.
Because we are in an area where it could go either way, I have to force myself to go down into lower time
frames to day trade the sessions. So, I'll be looking for inefficiencies and/or buy side or sell side liquidity for
draws on liquidity, and technically not have a bias intraday.

- BALANCED PRICE RANGE AND SELL SIDE TARGETS
On the S&P 15-minute chart:
On Sunday and Monday of last week, we rallied up, straight back down, then an aggressive rally higher,
and traded up into that higher inefficiency on the weekly chart (weekly volume imbalance). Then we worked
lower, traded back up into a Balanced Price Range. This occurs because of this singular up candle (a buy
side imbalance, sell side inefficiency
- BISI) and this singular candle (a sell side imbalance, buy side
inefficiency
- SIBI).
These two areas make this a Balanced Price Range. That's why we had it rally up and aggressively
slammed lower, targeting the sell side and trading into the Fair Value Gap right there, and then back up into
that same Balanced Price Range, extended to the right, boom, it rejects.

- SELL SIDE LIQUIDITY ATTRACTION
All of this consolidation here, in my mind, I'm thinking this is too enticing for them, and even this over here
is just too enticing for sell side liquidity. So, sell side liquidity here and sell side liquidity below these lows
here. I would favor a run into that in the first part of the week. That's what I would like to see.
But, I am again reminding you that we have not yet opened for the new week. Sunday afternoon is where
we're at at the time of me making this recording, and later in the evening, a few hours from now, we will see
the opening of the new week. So, unless we get above this high here, I think it warrants a probe of lower
sell side liquidity pools.

- SHORT-TERM BEARISH SETUP
On the NASDAQ e-mini Futures 15-minute time frame, I like the idea of getting below this low, and there's
that little bit of an inefficiency. As long as we don't trade above this high here, it looks heavy, and I would
favor a move to that sell side and closing in this inefficiency right there.
It's interesting to see how we had that little tiny Fair Value Gap right there; it went right up into it and then
slammed it lower for the sell side below here. It's rather shallow, and I traded this on Friday, but the
continuation lower is what I'm interested in.
While I wouldn't typically be afraid of it running above this short-term high and then collapsing, it's better
that it didn't do that because we have already, with the vision time frame, a really nice market structure
here. The fact that we went to a short-term premium there to get into this Fair Value Gap and Breaker.

- FAVORED SCENARIO AND WEEKLY TRADING PLAN
So I would like to see a gap lower, try to fill a gap, and reach into this area here. That's the scenario I'm
looking for for NASDAQ. It doesn't mean I'm going to be right; don't bet the farm on it, don't even try to take
a trade on it. Just observe it and watch it.
I won't be trading until we get to Tuesday. I want to use Monday's trading to provide me a better backdrop
or foundation for what I think the weekly candlestick is going to do, because as of right now, my mode of
thought going forward for this particular trading week is intraday only. No daily bias. That means we have to
trade intraday volatility, which means buy side or sell side liquidity and/or trading for inefficiencies, and
that's it.
CONCLUSION
- PATIENCE AND INTRADAY FOCUS
I know that's probably not what you want to hear because you just watched me deliver a really wonderful
month of July in terms of a big run for a swing trader model on a daily chart. But they don't set up every
day, they don't set up every week, and you have to sit on your hands.
That's what we're counseling you to do here today: sit still, relax, and let the next setup come to you, and
don't force it.
Again, as a reminder, this was just a review, and I'm just counseling you. I'm going very very slow with this
new week of trading and allowing Monday to give me the bias for the week. I'll talk to you next time. Be safe.
